description: Historic first Americas Region World Championship Qualifier. Five countries compete: USA, Canada, Jamaica, Brazil, and Argentina. Determines Americas region qualification spots for the 2027 World Championships in Leamington Spa, England. Silver tier World Bowls Series sanctioned ranking event.
Southwest Division mixed pairs tournament, first SWD event of the 2026 season.
Two-day Southwest Division men's rinks (fours) championship in Southern California.
Southwest Division women's California 5-bowl competition.
Annual carnival-style tournament at Long Beach, named in honor of Pat Gonzales.
Australian-format pairs tournament hosted by Sun City Lawn Bowling Club.
Southwest Division official mixed pairs championship.
Newport Classic mixed triples tournament at Newport Harbor Lawn Bowling Club.
Two-day Southwest Division men's singles championship named in honor of Murray-Allison.
Southwest Division women's veteran/novice triples, pairing experienced players with newcomers.

event_name: 2026 SWD Women's Heidi Rittner Australian Pairs
description: Southwest Division women's Australian format pairs tournament, named in honor of Heidi Rittner.
Annual Joe Siegman Pairs tournament at Beverly Hills Lawn Bowling Club with cash prizes.
24th edition of the Jack Beckley Friendship Tournament, a social competitive event celebrating the sport.
36th edition of the New York State Masters Championship, played across multiple weekends in April and May.
Jefferson Park Club team tournament. Teams of 5 compete in 3 twelve-end games per day over two days.
Jefferson Park Club two-day singles championship.
One-day mixed pairs open event. 4 games. Teams must include one player of each gender. Open to all NW Division members.
Two-day Jefferson Park pairs championship with separate men's and women's divisions.
Pairs combining novice and veteran bowlers. 4 games. Novice defined as anyone who has never bowled a game before 2023.
Senior singles open. 3 games plus playoff for top 4 players. For NW Division members turning 65 in 2026.
eligibility: NW Division members who turn 65 in 2026

description: Short-game singles format — first to 5 ends wins each match. Practice-focused competitive event with prizes for top 3.
Mixed triples with randomly drawn teams. 4 ten-end games.

dates.notes: Flexible completion; matches scheduled between July 5 and mid-September

description: 6th annual Rose Jug Singles Open. Double-elimination bracket tournament. Open to Portland area players with lawn bowling experience.
Veteran/novice pairs format requiring a veteran (started bowling before 2022) to partner with a novice (started 2022 or after).
eligibility: Veteran bowlers (pre-2022) must pair with novice bowlers (2022 or later)

dates.notes: Approximate — based on prior year pattern

description: Two-day Northwest Division pairs event with a modified format placing the jack at the 21-meter line.

Premier Bowls USA national championship event crowning US champions in men's and women's singles and pairs. All play in round-robin format. Singles matches to 21 shots; pairs play 18 ends. Participants must be US citizens who won their divisional playdown.
eligibility: Must be US citizen and win divisional qualifier (playdown)

location.club_name: Multiple clubs — Sun City and Sun City West

description: Bowls USA's premiere event welcoming hundreds of players from across the US and around the world. Open to all World Bowls affiliate members. Six competitive divisions: women's fours, pairs, singles; men's fours, pairs, singles.

description: Coaching clinic for vice and skip positions at Sarasota Lawn Bowling Club. Registration required through Cognito Forms.
Annual Sarasota LBC Club Championships with multiple tournament divisions for members.

description: Regular open bowling sessions at Clearwater Lawn Bowls Club. New bowlers welcome with free instruction and use of club bowls. Thursday evenings are under lights.

description: Regular bowling sessions and inter-club competitions during the winter season at Grand Lawn Bowling Club, Sun City AZ.

4-3-2-1 progressive format tournament at Berkeley Lawn Bowling Club, part of PIMD (Pacific Intermountain Division).

description: 2026 season at the historic New York Lawn Bowling Club in Central Park (founded 1926). Includes competitive summer league, several tournaments, and holiday social events.

gender: open (separate men's and women's plus mixed pairs)
description: 48th edition of the World Indoor Bowls Championships, organized by World Bowls Tour. Televised by BBC and World Bowls Tour. Robert Paxton (England) won open singles; Katherine Rednall (England) won ladies singles; Russell Bewick won the four-player masters.

gender: open (18 disciplines including Under-18, Over-60, Para, Intellectual Disability, Deaf/Hearing Impaired, Mixed Pairs)
description: 11th staging of the Australian Open on the Gold Coast. Mass-participation event with 3,550+ competitors over 14 days. First time including 18 disciplines with new Mixed Pairs, Intellectual Open Singles, and Deaf/Hearing Impaired Open Singles formats. Entry via BowlsLink account required.

location.club_name: City of Stirling — Osborne Park, North Beach, and Mount Lawley Bowling Clubs

description: 21-day national championships festival across three Perth venues comprising 6 competitions and 31 national titles: Australian Multi-Disability Championships, Champion of Champions, Under-18 Championships, Senior Sides Championships (over-60), Sides Championships (state teams), and Australian Championships.
Annual Australian Para Nationals lawn bowling championship for bowlers with physical disabilities.

42 competitions across 19 days at the home of English lawn bowls. Tens of thousands of spectators and thousands of competitors. Early bird tickets available until March 2026. Ticketing enquiries: ticketing@bowlsengland.com.
entry_fee: Varies — early bird general admission discounted until March 2026

description: Inter-county pairs competitions. The Balcomb Trophy for men and Walker Cup for women, played over preliminary and final rounds in May.
